subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the historical and cultural background of the Vijayanagara Empire
Analyse the character traits of Tenali Rama and King Krishnadeva Raya
Identify the conflict and resolution in the story
Understand how wit and humour can resolve misunderstandings
Develop critical thinking and communication skills through discussions and activities
Improve vocabulary and grammar through contextual exercises

lesson_plan.title: Understanding Wit and Wisdom
topics_covered: Importance of humour in solving problems
teaching_strategy: Discussion of riddles in the textbook
student_activities: List and explain qualities of a witty person

lesson_plan.title: Historical Background and Characters
topics_covered: Tenali Rama as a poet and advisor
teaching_strategy: Use of board diagrams to explain the court structure
student_activities: Identify the roles of important characters

lesson_plan.title: Conflict in the Story
student_activities: Explain the cause of the conflict
Discuss examples of misunderstandings in daily life

lesson_plan.title: Tenali Rama’s Problem-Solving Strategy
topics_covered: Presentation of the paddy seeds idea
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of the court scene
student_activities: Write a short explanation of Tenali Rama’s strategy

lesson_plan.title: Resolution and Moral Interpretation
topics_covered: Reconciliation between the king and queen
student_activities: Write the lesson learned from the story
Discuss how wisdom helps resolve conflicts

teacher_tip: Encourage students to analyse how Tenali Rama solved the problem indirectly. Discuss why using humour and intelligence can sometimes be more effective than direct confrontation.
